steps.title: Follow the contrast
steps.content: The text contrasts two uses of abstraction by Indigenous artists. Massey's Untitled is aligned with Abstract Expressionism, and few viewers would infer from its style that Massey was Indigenous. Earles is introduced in contrast because he builds abstract compositions from motifs common in traditional Caddo ceramics and graphic art.

steps.title: Make identity visible
steps.content: Because Earles uses traditional Caddo motifs, abstraction in his work makes Indigenous identity easier to recognize than it is in Massey's Untitled. Choice B invents Indigenous origins for Abstract Expressionist motifs. Choice C shifts to challenging European American dominance, which is not the stated effect. Choice D invents an uneasy reconciliation of influences in Massey's work. The answer is A.
